LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANTS UNION PAPER BOX & PRINTING PRESS, LIMITED, AND SEAWICK ENTERPRISES, LIMITED'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS- COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEMURRER SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART: 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION, CONTRACT CLAIMS, OVERRULED; 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION, BREACH OF WARRANTY, SUSTAINED, NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE CLAIMS AS STATED ARE BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS; 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION, NEGLIGENT INTERFERENCE, SUSTAINED; PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGED FACTS GIVING RISE TO A DUTY INDEPENDENT OF THE PERFORMANCE OF THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT; 6TH CAUSE, INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE, IS OVERRULED BASED ON PARAGRAPH 56; 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION, UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES, IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO CLARIFY WHAT IS MEANT BY "LICENSE DESIGN" TO ENABLE THE COURT AND THE PARTIES TO DETERMINE IF THE COPYRIGHT LAWS ARE APPLICABLE; 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION, ALTER EGO, PLEADS CONCLUSIONS, NOT FACTS TO SUPPORT SUCH A CLAIM, LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED; AND 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION, CONVERSION, OVERRULED. TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Off Calendar Feb-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Ronald A. Clifford, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: UNION PAPER BOX & PRINTING PRESS LIMITED and SEAWICK ENTERPRISES LIMITED
Timothy A. Dolan, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Artistic Studios LTD.

After hearing, PLAINTIFF UNION PAPER BOX & PRINTING PRESS LIMITED and SEAWICK ENTERPRISES LIMITED's Motion For Terminating Sanctions To: 1) Strike Answer Of Deft To The Amended Complaint; 2) Enter Default Against Deft And 3) Dismiss Third Amended X-Comp

Off Calendar Apr-09-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS-DEFENDANTS UNION PAPER BOX & PRINTING PRESS, LIMITED AND SEAWICK ENTERPRISES, LIMITED'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: RONALD A. CLIFFORD, ESQUIRE (BLAKELEY & BLAKELEY, 949.260.0622) APPEARING FOR CROSS-DEFENDANTS UNION PAPER BOX & PRINTING PRESS, LIMITED AND SEAWICK ENTERPRISES, LIMITED. THERE ARE NO OTHER APPEARANCES.
RULING: COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: OVERRULED, TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. PARAGRAPHS 39 THROUGH 42 AND 46 THROUGH 49 ALLEGE FACTS PLEADING A DUTY OF CARE APART FROM THE CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIP. THE FACTS ALSO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICE. THE ALTER EGO CLAIM ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS BASED ON INFORMATION AND BELIEF. THE CONVERSION CLAIM ALLEGES THAT ITS PROPERTY WAS CONVERTED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Ronald Clifford, Esq. of B & B, 949-260-0622 for plaintiff/cross-defendant and moving party Union Paper Box & Printing Press and Seawick Enterprises, Limited
John Dooling, Esq. of RMKB, 415-972-6319 for defendant/cross-complainant and opposing party Artistic Studios, LTD

RULING:
ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING:
CROSS-DEFENDANTS UNION PAPER BOX & PRINTING PRESS, LIMITED, AND SEAWICK ENTERPRISES, LIMITED'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS- COMPLAINT: Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part: 1st and 2nd causes of action, contract claims, overruled; 3rd and 4th causes of action, breach of warranty, sustained, no opposition filed and the claims as stated are barred by the statute of limitations; 5th cause of action, negligent interference, sustained; plaintiff is given leave to amend to alleged facts giving rise to a duty independent of the performance of the terms of the contract; 6th cause, intentional interference, is overruled based on paragraph 56; 7th cause of action, unfair business practices, is sustained with leave to amend to clarify what is meant by "license design" to enable the court and the parties to determine if the copyright laws are applicable; 8th cause of action, alter ego, pleads conclusions, not facts to support such a claim, leave to amend is granted; and 9th cause of action, conversion, overruled. Ten days leave to amend is granted. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600 (302/PJM/VC)
